comments: In adult Heard near threipmuir reservoir and a noisy juvenile Heard by Bonaly reservoir.
Heard but did not see it.
must darker bird than the ones seen recently
Distinctive red shield and bicolored red bill with yellow tip. Foraging along shore. Poor iPhone photo.
Seen together in flight. Both landed nearby.
Perching in hillside thicket. Active. Moving around
Rich whistle calls.  One bright male seen.
Bobbing in the waves. Young male.
Stuttering low flight over rocky shoreline. Wing stripe visible in flight. Bold eyering. Eyeline.
Breeding plumage. Black hood, noticeable white eye arcs, bright orange-red bill and leges. Larger than Back-headed Gull.
Breeding plumage. Smallish yellow bill. Dark eyes. Low flyover allowed observation of features. Pale gray mantle.
Black cap, black bill with yellow tip.
Nice binocular views. Flying and diving offshore.
Flew east just off sea wall.
Including one juvenile that was snitched by a gull. It was pretty brutal, especially as the parents furiously attacked the gull while it swallowed it whole.
Both juveniles confident enough to explore the surrounding branches around the nest. They'll probably fledge in the next few days.
Mostly Graylag in appearance, but has visible (if faint) white chinstrap resembling CANG.

Accipiter suddenly dove into brush after prey along nearby brushy hillside as I watched at fairly close range. Unsuccessful. Flew out with no prey and flew to top of ridge and perched in small tree. Molting tail feathers. Long gray tail with alternating light gray/dark gray bands. Horizontal barring on breast and belly. Pale golden eyes. Grayscale coloration suggests female. High-pitched calls. Horrible iPhone photo taken of bird perching at top of ridge.
Repeated notes. Briefly in view of
Calling back and forth in two locations. One female perching in view. Poor iPhone photo taken.
